One-sided Hurting

Ta hurling Cork faced Roscommon with
‘abran new team on which very i. familiar
faces were to be seen, Blackrock, Dun-
gourney, “and other notable Cork teams
were quite unrepresented, Midleton supply=
ing the players, with one each from Sars-
fields and Redmonds. *In the circumstances
it would-scareely be faic to judge the team
as representative of Cork ‘hurling talent,
‘The opposition of Roscommon ‘was easily
disposed of as Cork bad always command
ofthe situation. . In the opening stages the
Roscommon goal-keeper distinguishes him-
Seif by balliant saving, but Williams and
Gorman broke through for a goal cach
Waile Byrne skied two points, and O'Leary
fone. ‘Khe only performance of note by
Roscommon was a nice run on the left
wing which culminated in a goal per Bren-
“han. At the interval Cork led by 3-3 to
1-0.

Cork improved their style after the
resumption and were very aggressive. A
very creditable defence was put by the Ros-
common goalkeeper, but Williatns, Egan
fand Walsh were not to be denied and they
added to the scores. Byrne stood out on his
‘own in the Cork ranks and his centre-
field work was very effeétive. The Ros-
common custodian also deserves mention
tor his fine defence—but he should be care~
ful to keep neater his goalposts, and leave
the backs to do their own particular work,
‘Lhe Binal score was

Cork = ‘5 goals 5 points
Roscommon 1 1pal 2 points
Leltrim's Good Display

Te was verging onf darkness when the
Cork-Leitrim fc ie jas concluded,
which Cork fer’ Wh interesting

struggle. ‘The Leitin boys showed sur-
prisingly good form, and expectations of an
sy Win foc Cork were exploded. after the

leather had’been thrown in, Heslin and
Py McGorern were prominent in x good,
strajegic attack on the Cork posts, and
despite the opposition of *M. Mehign

ourse of which Heslin struck the Cork

fore the interval, Heslia had penetraced the

another goal for’ Cork. Half-time score—

Leitria’ re-firted wo fight down theie

but the defence was good. However, they
Secured aipoint per Helin, to whieh

which Donovan and Cotter negotiated for

injuries, Cork held the uppertians to the
finish, and won on the , aal score —Cork,
2 goals 3 points; Leitim, 4 poi

Comments

Leitrim were, on the whole, vey un-
lucky, and they inst be complimented upon
their display against a crack team. ‘heie
catching was very good, but Cork "fielded
ever sy much bewer. ‘The game was
keenly contested, but in a perfe ndly
spirit. For the winners Mebigan and
Herlihy were in their usual good form,
and they ate a really first-class pair of fulls.
Power caught the eye frequently in ezutre~
ficid, while Donovan, Cotter and McGarvey
were best amongst the forward», For
Leitrim Phelin “aMcGovern was an ‘out-
standing Sgute, his place-cicking ocing un-
usually good. ‘Reynolds ana J. Fiynn were

Beautifal weather favoured the event
and a fairly large gathering of enthusiastic
fans cheered the combatants, who fought
out the issue. in a good, friendly spirit.
James Scanlan, captain of the Ranger Foot-
ball Club, gave entice satisfaction as re-
eee.

The balance between the teams is very
little, as was demonstrated by the closeness
of the score up to two minutes ‘of full time,
Both teams played'a fine, manly game. The
backs were particulacly ‘brilliant, ingerton,
Aner, Burn», and McCarthy coing fine
work for che Young eelaads. Young was
always safe in the forward line, while che
centre was safe in the hands of Firagerald,
Fahey, and Walsh. For the: O'Coanells
the custodian did ae behind the brill
back line composed of Rois, Cobo, Murphy
and Kelly, all of whom distinguished theme
selves, Egan wasalways reliable at centre,
and in the forward live Elaherty and Cam=
pion were always dangerous.

LOOKER”

‘The event for: which enthusiasts. have
been hungering for almost a month past is
now at han. Kerry and Wexford mect

Sunday Beside this. momentous

happening, all other Gaclic concerns shrink

said about these lamous combinations.
‘They met previously only a few weeks since,
and the greatest Gaelic football game of all

«time resulted in a draw. Some say Wex-
ford should have won, while others give the
palm toKerry, But what's the use? The
issue is stil knit. Both sides have still an
opportunity of working out their salvatio

[eis uncertain whether anyone” ‘has
taken into consideration ‘the probable line
of action which willl be taken should the
match result in another draw at full time.
‘The match should, I think, be played to &
finish, if time and daylight allows.

People should not be inclined to jump at
conclusions based upon what occurred dur-

ig the previousencounter. Both counties
have occupied the interval to make such
changes and improvements as are calculated
to flabbergast and pulverise the oppesition,
With. such forces as these at work, all
speculation im cégard to the svent becomes
mere gucss-work,

them to field perfectly trained men, able to

level, I 5

BELFAST NOTES

‘The putstanding event. of the, week was
the sensational triumph of Crowleys over
Sarsfields, Ic was the ‘sequel ro a well-
planned “playing system, the tactics Irom
beginning to end being too much for their
opponents. The Sarsfelds. were strongly
represented, whilst, surprising to relate,
Crowleys lacked the service of three very
‘apable players. ‘Their success unser the
circumstances is deserving of great praise.

Tn the fist half the defence of Crowleys
was subjected’to a great deal of pressure,
but stood the test well, Murphy and
O'Toole coming to the rescue oF their side
time without number, Sarsfelds kept up
a heavy bomburdment, but their efforts t0
defeat Connolly proved unavailing, the cus-
todiany repelling shots of every variety and
from all angles. At half-time they had put
to their account two points, while Crowleys
had scored a great goal per Murphy at forty
arés range.

Crowleys’ centee-forward,, managed to put
over point, which he supplemented shortly
afterwards by two more. Sarsfelds strove
might and main to reduce the lead, and it
was no disgrace to the Crowleys ‘when a
point was recorded against them. The
Fish of a thrilling game found the score:
Crowleys 1 goal 3 points (6 pts)
Sarsfelds 3 points

The winnets were “all” to the fore in
the great achievement. Connolly kept the
goal finely. The outstanding man, how.
ever, was Murphy. He is a “kicking”
genivg bis goal being a beauty. Gareagan
and Burns, backed up by the halves, were
a much superior line to those of the epposi>
tion. The latter were best served by
Armstrong, Coburn, ‘Burns and Ward.
The rest, however, although very deter-
mined, were not so happy in the results
that attended their efforts,

Shauns, after a very fine exposition of
Gaelic football, won over Ardoyne: Both
defences were simply great, but the weak-
ness of the ‘“North-End ” forwards helped
materially in their defeat. They can, if
placed in their proper position, render a
more efficient account of themselves, Why

iment he progressed to the extent of

paired his health, and after a very brief

——_ DUBLIN

Cavan

Cavan County Committee (Junior) —
With a view to the selection of a represen
tative team to play Dublin at Croke Park
fon January 17th it was decided that players
from Nos. « and 3 Divisions meet at Cros-
serlough at 2.30 on Sunday 6th December,
Mr. McEnrog, referee. ‘The winners to
play No. 2 Division on December 20th
on a venue to'be arranged. It was decided
that teams desirous of joining the Inter-
mediate League communicate at once with
the Secretary, Mr. R, Stuart, Ballinagh.

Medals—The runners-up, Crosserlough,
were presented with a set of medals.

Annual Convention—The annul con.
vention was fixed for second Sunday in
January,
Mayo

A credit balance of £20, county cham-
pionships concluded, and a satisfactory out-
look for the coming season, confeonted the
delegates to the Mayo county convention
which was held in Claremorris. ‘The
secretary stared in his report that Mr.
Taylor, Castlebar, had acted contrary to
a the withes of the County Board in ene

deavouring to field a team against Galway
fon the same date as a Volunteer review was
being held in Ballina. In connection with
the matter, the following resolution was
passed :—

“That we, the members of the Mayo
County Board of the G.A.A. in annual
Convention assembled, condemn the action
of Mr. Taylor, Castlebar, in trying to
get up a team in Mayo to play Galway in
Roscommon on the 2and_ August la
behind the back of the Mayo County
Board of the G.A.A.”

Messrs. P. McManus (Swinford), chair=
man ; Mr. P. Kilduff (Ballina), sectetary j
and Mc. R. Walsh (Balla), treasurer, were
Unanimously re-appointed to their rsspece
tive positions.

Tn order to train Mayo Junior football
team drawa to meet Ker’y in the Alle
Ireland Junior semi-final, a test match was
fixed for 13th December at Swinford,

Ic was decided to institute minor county
championships for teams under 17 years of
age.
